next_action: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether future co-op is capped at two players, targets up to four players but may stage the first implementation around two-player validation, or requires full four-player support from the first co-op MVP. Preserve post-MVP co-op status, fixed mission difficulty, active-player-count encounter scaling, personal non-replenishable difficulty-based lives, spectator clutch play, separate co-op progression, host continuity, teleport-based late joining as a later goal, solo-lead production, and Unity with C#. Include social appeal, encounter readability, projectile and effect density, camera and room constraints, UI, network bandwidth and synchronization, host migration, testing combinations, matchmaking population, implementation cost, and risk of architecture that cannot later expand. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. Queue D-191 through D-200 and persist at D-200 unless the user changes the batching rule or requests a context handoff.
